blocks.name: Transparency
blocks.description: Recommendation systems are 'white boxes', and explain to users why the recommendation was made. This provides at least partial insight into the inner logic of the system.

blocks.name: Justification
blocks.description: An explanation for the recommendations is given to the user, but without mentioning how they were chosen or how the system or how the system works. The system is therefore a 'black box' for users.

blocks.name: Controllability
blocks.description: Involvement of users by providing input and feedback to the system and thus improving the accuracy of the recommendations. For example, ratings are given or preferences are specified.

blocks.name: Cold Start
blocks.description: Effective recommendations can only be made when data about the user is known. Often those systems have an inital choose of Elements they like, so that the system can start.

blocks.name: Explorability
blocks.description: Explorability means navigating through information spaces, the viewing of new recommendations, and/or the exploration of the information space.

blocks.name: Novelty
blocks.description: Novelty refers to a recommended item that is new and 'different' for the user.

blocks.name: Diversity
blocks.description: Diversity, in the context of a recommendation list, is a broad spectrum of information.

blocks.name: Serendipity
blocks.description: Serendipity means serendipitous and delightful discoveries. Serendipity occurs when unexpected circumstances and an 'aha moment' lead to a valuable, unexpected result. unexpected result.

blocks.name: Predict
blocks.description: Assessment whether task can be mastered

blocks.name: Sort
blocks.description: Sort Data Set e.g. by Drag & Drop Action

blocks.name: Filter
blocks.description: Reduce Data Set by e.g. button

blocks.name: Select
blocks.description: The inclusion or exclusion of data

blocks.name: Compare
blocks.description: Comparison of data or analysis of data stock e.g. by clustering and showing correlations

blocks.name: Highlight
blocks.description: Describes a selection which changes an element, e.g. by a frame or a color

blocks.name: Weight Features
blocks.description: Weighting in recommender systems gives an understanding of the relative importance of the features of a user profile.

blocks.name: Rate
blocks.description: Explicit ratings can be given by e.g. double-clicking or swiping, these are used to collect user preferences; they can be collected via a rating scale (e.g. star icons), binary (e.g. thumb up/down) or stand-alone (e.g. Thumb up/Down)

blocks.name: Adjust
blocks.description: By changing parameters

blocks.name: Show More/Less
blocks.description: More or leses information about a particular element is shown

blocks.name: Search
blocks.description: Reduce result list by searching

blocks.name: Explore
blocks.description: Discover and navigate through dataset

blocks.name: Set Mark
blocks.description: To place a marker on a map or a geographic map

blocks.name: Individual
blocks.description: Information observed from independent entities (e.g., users or items) that may share common features, e.g. weather information, user behavior and preference, configurations, groups of elements with common features

blocks.name: Time
blocks.description: Corresponds to information such as time of the day, current time, day of the week, and season of the year

blocks.name: Location
blocks.description: Refers to the location associated with the activity of an entity, this location may be physical (e.g. coordinates of the current location, address of a movie theater) or virtual (e.g. IP address of a computer)

blocks.name: Relational
blocks.description: Refers to entity relationships that arise from the circumstances in which the entities are involved e.g. interpersonal relationships

blocks.name: Activity
blocks.description: Refers to the tasks performed by entities at a particular time e.g. shopping

blocks.name: Entertainment
blocks.description: Domains like music or movie

blocks.name: News
blocks.description: Domain News, for example articles

blocks.name: Education
blocks.description: E-Learning domain

blocks.name: Tourism
blocks.description: Domains like Travel or Food

blocks.name: Social Network
blocks.description: Social Media data

blocks.name: E-Commerce
blocks.description: Concerns domains related to buying and selling and sales transactions on the Internet

blocks.name: Single Result
blocks.description: Result Element in System

blocks.name: Tile
blocks.description: Graphical unit(s) in a system, which are e.g. arranged in a grid

blocks.name: Metaphoric Map
blocks.description: For example terrain illustrations, which differ in essential characteristics from the conventional cartographic representations of the surface of the earth

blocks.name: Process Chart
blocks.description: Is the graphical form of the process representation

blocks.name: List
blocks.description: Denotes an enumeration of elements

blocks.name: Geographic Map
blocks.description: Represents geographical areas or regions represents

blocks.name: Scatter Plot
blocks.description: Are used to display correlations or to compare attributes, e.g. the rating of users, correlations between recommendations between recommendations and ratings

blocks.name: Node-Link Diagram
blocks.description: For the representation of connections between elements with connecting lines and nodes representing data about users

blocks.name: Bar Chart
blocks.description: Is a chart that represents data with rectangular bars, the height or length of which is proportional to the values they represent

blocks.name: Tree Map
blocks.description: Is used to visualize hierarchical structures, which are represented by nested rectangles

blocks.name: Cluster
blocks.description: Are grouped areas that visualize the context

blocks.name: Sankey Diagram
blocks.description: Is a graphical representation of quantity flows, in contrast to the flow chart, quantities are represented by quantity-proportional thick arrows

blocks.name: Point
blocks.description: Is a zero-dimensional (0D) marker

blocks.name: Text
blocks.description: Readable characters

blocks.name: Line
blocks.description: Is a one-dimensional (1D) marker

blocks.name: Icon
blocks.description: Visualizes processes, product features or objects

blocks.name: Thumbnail
blocks.description: Preview image

blocks.name: Glyph
blocks.description: Concrete graphical or typometric representation of a character or or character within a writing system

blocks.name: Area
blocks.description: Is a two-dimensional (2D) marker

blocks.name: Search View
blocks.description: View with filters to narrow down the result set

blocks.name: Result View
blocks.description: For the presentation of result

blocks.name: Control View
blocks.description: View for setting parameters such as user preferences

blocks.name: Position
blocks.description: Describes the absolute position, for example on a Map

blocks.name: Size
blocks.description: Specifies how much space a label or symbol takes up

blocks.name: Tilt
blocks.description: Or also angle

blocks.name: Shape
blocks.description: Is the shape of an element e.g. star

blocks.name: Color Gradient
blocks.description: Specifies a set of position-dependent colors that are normally used to fill an area

blocks.name: Color Hue
blocks.description: Is a characteristic or property of a color

blocks.name: Pan/Scroll
blocks.description: Panning (2D) or scrolling (1D)

blocks.name: Drag
blocks.description: A way to operate user interfaces by moving graphical elements

blocks.name: Swipe
blocks.description: Combining pointing device or finger movements and clicks to trigger an computer event

blocks.name: Tap
blocks.description: User control to select an object of interest

blocks.name: Spread/Pinch
blocks.description: Gesture e.g. Zoom

blocks.name: Hover
blocks.description: Mouseover control, e.g. to display metadata

blocks.name: Slider
blocks.description: Voting between different expressions

blocks.name: Button
blocks.description: Icon, text, image or button that acts as a link

blocks.name: Input
blocks.description: Data input via UI-Element

blocks.name: Dropdown
blocks.description: Is a control element of a graphical user interface, with which a user can select a value from a given list of values

blocks.name: Toggle
blocks.description: Change Dataset, eg. between most recommended and least recommended

blocks.name: Overlay
blocks.description: Is a small pop-up window in application programs or web pages, which shows a description of an element of the user interface
